available_commands.description: Build a continuation prompt that captures current progress, decisions, and file references so a fresh conversation can resume without searching. Use when wrapping up a session, switching context, or when the user says "checkpoint", "continuation prompt", "wrap up session", or "save context". (user)
available_commands.name: checkpoint

available_commands.description: Bootstrap a fresh session from a checkpoint continuation prompt. Use at the start of a new conversation to resume work from where the last session left off. Invoke with /recap [slug]. (user)
available_commands.name: recap

available_commands.description: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. (dynamic workflow)
available_commands.name: deep-research

available_commands.description: (frontend-design) Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
available_commands.name: frontend-design

available_commands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
available_commands.name: update-config

available_commands.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
available_commands.name: verify

available_commands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues
available_commands.name: debug

available_commands.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
available_commands.name: code-review

available_commands.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
available_commands.name: simplify

available_commands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.
available_commands.name: batch

available_commands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
available_commands.name: fewer-permission-prompts

available_commands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
available_commands.name: loop

available_commands.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule.
available_commands.name: schedule

available_commands.description:
Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
available_commands.name: claude-api

available_commands.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
available_commands.name: run

available_commands.description: Author or improve the run-<unit> skill — a per-project skill that tells agents how to build, launch, and drive this project's app. Use when the user asks to set up the project, get it running, write run instructions, or verify build/run steps work from a clean environment.
available_commands.name: run-skill-generator

available_commands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far
available_commands.name: compact

available_commands.description: Show current context usage
available_commands.name: context

available_commands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop
available_commands.name: heapdump

available_commands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
available_commands.name: init

available_commands.description: Pick up skills added or changed on disk during this session
available_commands.name: reload-skills

available_commands.description: Review a pull request
available_commands.name: review

available_commands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
available_commands.name: security-review

available_commands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session
available_commands.name: usage

available_commands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
available_commands.name: insights

available_commands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met
available_commands.name: goal

available_commands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
available_commands.name: team-onboarding
